overview: These birds are White-tailed Kites, a species of medium-sized raptor found in grasslands and savannas. They are well-known for "kiting," a hunting behavior where they hover on rapidly beating wings over open fields to spot small rodents. While they have fierce features like hooked beaks and sharp talons, their plumage is uniquely bright white and gray, sometimes causing them to resemble gulls. This specific image captures a dramatic aerial exchange of a vole, a common behavior when feeding young. Los Padres ForestWatch +5 6 sites White-tailed Kite | Hovering Raptor
